---
title: "RaCiNG"
author: "Marcelo Hurtado"
date: "2026-03-25"
output: html_document
---

Load input
```{r}
source("RaCInG_input_generation.R")

# Call the function
res <- generateInput("min", "SKCM", folder = "~/Documents/RaCInG/Python_Code/Example input")

# Extract variables (same as Python unpacking)
Lmatrix   <- res$CellLigList
Rmatrix   <- res$CellRecList
Cdistr    <- res$Dtypes
LRdistr   <- res$DconnectionTensor
cellTypes <- res$celltypes
ligs      <- res$ligands
recs      <- res$receptors
```

Network generation
```{r}
source("network_generation.R")
# -------------------------------------------------------------
# Estimated running time: less than 1 second
# -------------------------------------------------------------

set.seed(1)  # make results reproducible

# Use only patient 1 (index 1 in R) from your input distributions
patient_index <- 1

# Subset distributions for this patient
Dcelltype_patient <- Cdistr[patient_index, ]          # cell type probabilities
Dligrec_patient <- LRdistr[, , patient_index]         # ligand-receptor probabilities

# Generate a small graph for demo
graph <- model1(
  N = 20,        # number of cells
  avdeg = 2,     # average degree
  cellLigList = Lmatrix,
  cellRecList = Rmatrix,
  Dcelltype = Dcelltype_patient,
  Dligrec = Dligrec_patient,
  genRandom = FALSE
)

# Extract results
V <- graph$V        # cell types of vertices
E <- graph$E        # edge list
types <- graph$types # ligand-receptor types for each edge

# Print results
print("Vertex types:")
print(V)
print("Edge list:")
print(E)
print("Ligand-Receptor types per edge:")
print(types)
```

Generate uniform LRdistr 
```{r}
source("network_generation.R")
graphData <- generateUniformLRGraph(LRdistr, Lmatrix, Rmatrix, Cdistr, cellTypes, patient = 1, N = 20, avdeg = 2)
```

Features extraction
```{r}
source("Utilities.R")
# Convert edge list to adjacency matrices
Adj <- EdgetoAdj(E, length(V))          # retains self-loops
AdjNoLoop <- EdgetoAdj_No_loop(E, length(V))  # removes self-loops

# Print results
cat("The adjacency matrix with self loops is:\n")
print(Adj)  # self-loops are retained

cat("The adjacency matrix without self loops is:\n")
print(AdjNoLoop)  # self-loops removed
```

```{r}
source("feature_extraction.R")
# Compute features using your R implementations
tt_res <- Trust_Triangles(AdjNoLoop)
ct_res <- Cycle_Triangles(AdjNoLoop)
w_res  <- Wedges(AdjNoLoop)

# Extract values
NoTT <- tt_res$NoTriangles
listTT <- tt_res$Triangle_list

NoCT <- ct_res$NoTriangles
listCT <- ct_res$Triangle_list

NoW <- w_res$NoWedges
listW <- w_res$Wedge_list

# Print results
cat("Number of trust triangles:", NoTT, ". List of trust triangles:\n")
print(listTT)  # Should start with c(2, 12, 14)

cat("Number of cycle triangles:", NoCT, ". List of cycle triangles:\n")
print(listCT)  # Should be empty if none exist

cat("Number of wedges:", NoW, ". List of wedges:\n")
print(listW)   # Should start with c(2, 9, 11)
```

```{r}
# Count wedges by cell-type combinations
typeCount <- Count_Types(oblist = listW, V = V)

# Print the resulting array of counts
print(typeCount)
```

Monte Carlo
```{r}
source("Monte_Carlo_Method.R")
# ------------------------------------------------------------
# Set random seed for reproducibility
# ------------------------------------------------------------
set.seed(1)

# ------------------------------------------------------------
# Run Monte Carlo simulation for wedges for one patient
# ------------------------------------------------------------
x = runSimOne(
  cancer_type = "SKCM",
  weight_type = "min",
  communication_type = "W",   # "W" = wedges
  pat = 1,                    # patient index
  N = 11,                   # number of cells
  itNo = 2,                  # number of Monte Carlo graphs
  av = 3,                    # average degree
  norm = FALSE,               # whether to normalize interaction distribution
  folder = "~/Documents/RaCInG/Python_Code/Example input"    # folder with input data
)

runSim(
  cancer_type = "SKCM",
  weight_type = "min",
  communication_type = "W",
  pats = 2,
  N = 11,
  itNo = 2,
  av = 3,
  norm = FALSE,
  folder = "~/Documents/RaCInG/Python_Code/Example input"
)

runSim(
  cancer_type = "SKCM",
  weight_type = "min",
  communication_type = "W",
  pats = 2,
  N = 11,
  itNo = 2,
  av = 3,
  norm = TRUE,
  folder = "~/Documents/RaCInG/Python_Code/Example input"
)
```

```{r}
source("txt_to_csv.R")
# Call the function in R
Generate_normalised_count_csv(
  cancer_type = "SKCM",
  weight_type = "min",
  triangle_types = "W",
  average = 3,
  noCells = 1000,
  folder = "~/Documents/RaCInG/Python_Code/Example input",
  remove_direction = TRUE
)
```
